briefing.opening: Good morning/afternoon, everyone. We will now commence the climate briefing for July 14, 2026.

sections.title: 🔴 Unprecedented Climate Catastrophe: Heatwaves, Wildfires, and Threats to Life
sections.content: Globally, heatwaves and wildfires are causing numerous casualties, bringing the climate catastrophe into stark reality. In Europe, over 10,000 excess deaths were reported due to heatwaves within a single month [12, 38, 57]. In countries such as the UK, France, and Spain, heatwaves provided ideal conditions for wildfires, leading to widespread large-scale fires, causing loss of life and evacuations [11, 69, 78, 81, 82, 83]. Domestically, the situation is no exception; immediately following heatwaves approaching 40 degrees Celsius, a volatile monsoon season has begun, with heavy rainfall of 50mm per hour expected [3, 39, 40]. Notably, the number of 119 emergency dispatches for heat-related illnesses has quadrupled in the last four years, and 115 heat-related illness patients were reported in Yeongdong, North Chungcheong Province, in a single day, indicating a severe health threat from heatwaves [41]. These phenomena vividly demonstrate the direct and destructive impacts of climate change on our society and lives.

sections.title: ⚠️ Crisis in Ecosystems and Food Security
sections.content: Climate change poses a severe threat not only to human life but also to the entire global ecosystem. Domestically, the Hwangbangsan toad is facing a double challenge from climate change and roadkill [2]. The Pacific gray whale population is warned to face catastrophic collapse, with numbers plummeting by half due to food loss caused by the climate crisis [10]. Internationally, Alaska's iconic fish species are being destroyed by invasive predators [42], and the ecological balance is being disrupted as owls are forced to hunt different prey due to climate change [59]. Furthermore, concerns about food security and biodiversity are growing, with crop nutrients being threatened, and 30% of plant species used in traditional Parmesan cheese and by Amazonian indigenous peoples being endangered by climate change [46, 49, 75]. According to research, farmers are already incurring losses of over 20 billion dollars annually due to global warming, and there are warnings that water utility fees in some cities could double by mid-century [71, 77].

sections.title: 🌱 Domestic and International Efforts and Achievements to Overcome the Climate Crisis
sections.content: Amidst this severe climate crisis, various efforts are actively underway both domestically and internationally to respond to climate change and achieve carbon neutrality. Gwangju Metropolitan City, Jeollanam-do, plans to expand its nectar forests by 3,825 hectares to combat climate change [1]. In Taean County, region-specific adaptation efforts are notable, such as the commencement of pilot aquaculture for alternative clam species [5]. Korea Water Resources Corporation (K-water) is strengthening climate crisis response and water cooperation with the Global Green Growth Institute (GGGI) and actively seeking international green projects [6, 7]. Cheorwon County has completed a smart irrigation system pilot project, presenting a new model for climate change-responsive agriculture [8]. Citizen participation in achieving carbon neutrality is also active; in Gwangmyeong City, nearly 20,000 'Climate Volunteers' (기후의병) are acting as 'carbon neutrality evangelists' [14], and in Gwacheon City, a citizen-participatory 'Carbon Neutrality Academy' is now fully operational [17]. In the energy transition sector, SK Group and KKR have launched the largest renewable energy platform in Korea [32]. Voluntary movements are spreading, such as non-regular workers from coal power plants facing job loss establishing the nation's first renewable energy cooperative [26, 29]. Furthermore, technological innovations and active corporate participation, including the development of CO2 capture technology from the air using new electrochemical devices [76] and Lenovo's declaration of 'over 90% renewable electricity' [34], are sending positive signals for overcoming the climate crisis.

sections.title: 🌍 The Complex Reality and Policy Challenges of Energy Transition
sections.content: Energy transition, a core component of climate crisis response, continues to face complex realities and policy challenges. While climate finance support from multilateral development banks has reached an all-time high, the World Bank's retreat has raised red flags for developing country support targets [9]. In Germany, the debate over postponing the carbon neutrality deadline is intensifying, leading to a direct clash between industry and the Green Party [20]. The South Korean government has officially announced its consideration of additional nuclear power plants, exploring an energy policy that combines nuclear with renewable energy [25]. Meanwhile, in Europe, a cultural barrier against installing air conditioners even during 40-degree Celsius heatwaves is posing a challenge to climate response [4]. Changes in the market are also being observed in renewable energy projects, such as TotalEnergies selling its European distributed solar assets [33]. Research and discussions on various aspects and impacts of climate change are also ongoing, including how climate change affects baseball flight distances [43] and the interaction between climate change and indigenous peoples in shaping Brazil's highland forests [50]. As such, energy transition is intricately linked with technological, economic, socio-cultural, and political factors, requiring a comprehensive and balanced approach.

briefing.closing: Today's briefing has once again underscored the severity of the climate catastrophe, while also highlighting the diverse efforts being made to overcome it, alongside the complex challenges that still lie ahead. We must continue to address the climate crisis wisely through sustained attention and cooperation. This concludes today's climate briefing.

articles.original_title: 폭염에 세계 곳곳 신음···유럽 1만명 초과사망·미국도 초비상
articles.description: On the 13th (local time), citizens gathered at the beach in Douarnenez, Brittany, northwestern France, to escape the heat and enjoy swimming. AFP Yonhap News. Heatwave-related damages are emerging in various parts of the world. Europe has reported over 10,000 excess deaths, and 58 million people in the United States are under heatwave advisories. According to The Guardian and Reuters on the 12th (local time), a heatwave has swept across the European continent...

summary.phenomenon: Since the 12th (local time), heatwave damages have been reported in various parts of the world, with over 10,000 excess deaths in Europe and 58 million people in the United States affected by the heatwave.
summary.cause: This is due to a heatwave that has swept across the European continent.

articles.original_description: 13일(현지시간) 프랑스 북서부 브르타뉴지방의 두아르네네즈의 해변에 더위를 피하기 위해 모인 시민들이 해수욕을 즐기고 있다. AFP연합뉴스세계 곳곳에서 폭염에 따른 피해가 속출하고 있다. 유럽에는 1만명 넘는 초과 사망자가 발생했고, 미국에서는 5800만명이 폭염 영향권에 들었다.12일(현지시간) 가디언과 로이터 통신 등에 따르면 폭염이 유럽 대륙을 덮친 ···

articles.title: Heatwave and Heavy Rain Arrive Simultaneously... Daytime Highs of '37°C' and '50mm' of Hourly Rainfall from Night
articles.original_title: 폭염·폭우 함께 왔다···낮 최고기온 ‘37도’ 무더위·밤부터 시간당 ‘50㎜’ 폭우
articles.description: On the 12th, as the first 'Severe Heatwave Warning' was issued in Gyeongsan and Pohang cities, Gyeongbuk Province, amidst nationwide sweltering heat, citizens sought relief in the water at Yeouido Hangang Park swimming pool in Seoul. Reporter Kwon Do-hyun. On Tuesday, the 14th, the nation will be generally cloudy with rain. Daytime high temperatures are expected to reach 37°C. According to the Korea Meteorological Administration's forecast, rain that began in the Seoul metropolitan area and Chungnam in the morning will spread to other central regions and Jeolla Province in the afternoon...

summary.phenomenon: On the 12th, the first 'Severe Heatwave Warning' was issued in Gyeongsan and Pohang cities, Gyeongbuk Province. On the 14th, nationwide sweltering heat with daytime highs reaching 37°C is expected, along with heavy rainfall of 50mm per hour from the night.
summary.outlook: The Korea Meteorological Administration forecasts that rain, which began in the Seoul metropolitan area and Chungnam in the morning of the 14th, will spread to other central regions and Jeolla Province in the afternoon.

articles.original_description: 전국적 무더위 속에 경북 경산시와 포항시에 첫 ‘폭염중대경보’가 발령된 지난 12일 서울 여의도 한강공원 수영장을 찾은 시민들이 물속에서 더위를 식히고 있다. 권도현 기자화요일인 14일 전국이 대체로 흐리고 비가 오겠다. 낮 최고기온은 37도까지 오르겠다.기상청 예보를 보면 이날 오전 수도권과 충남에 오던 비가 오후부터 그 밖 중부지방과 전라권으로 번지겠다···

articles.title: “Fruits Might Get Sunburn from the Blazing Sun”… Farmers' Anguish Amidst 40-Degree Scorching Heat
articles.original_title: “불볕에 과일 화상 입을라”…40도 끓는 더위에 애끓는 농심
articles.description: Visiting Pohang under a 'Severe Heatwave Warning': Rural areas in 'emergency' due to apple sunburn damage, etc. Elderly farmers advised to refrain from outdoor activities via text message. Village chiefs in Gigye-myeon conducting patrols. Number of vacationers halved due to scorching sun. POSCO strengthens work environment support. “I can manage to water them, but I’m worried the apples will get scorched by the sun.” Han Sang-soo (68) bent under an apple tree in his orchard in Gigye-myeon, Buk-gu, Pohang, Gyeongbuk Province, on the 13th, operating a sprinkler...

summary.phenomenon: On the 13th, agricultural damage, including sunburn on apples, is occurring in rural areas such as Gigye-myeon, Buk-gu, Pohang, Gyeongbuk Province, due to scorching heat reaching 40°C.
summary.cause: This is due to the ongoing scorching heat reaching 40°C.
summary.outlook: Rural areas are on high alert, with text messages advising elderly farmers to refrain from outdoor activities and village-by-village patrols being conducted. The number of vacationers has also halved.

articles.original_description: ‘폭염중대경보’ 포항 가보니사과 일소피해 등 농촌 ‘비상’ 고령 농민 야외활동 자제 문자 기계면선 이장들 마을별 순찰 뙤약볕 탓 피서객도 절반 ‘뚝’ 포스코, 작업 환경 지원 강화“물이야 내가 어떻게든 주면 되는데, 사과가 햇볕에 데버릴까봐 그게 걱정이지.”한상수씨(68)가 13일 경북 포항시 북구 기계면의 사과밭에서 나무 아래로 허리를 숙인 채 스프링클러···

articles.title: 119 Emergency Dispatches for Heat-Related Illnesses Quadrupled in 4 Years
articles.original_title: 온열질환 119 출동, 4년 새 4배 뛰었다
articles.description: The proportion of elderly patients aged 65 or older reached its highest. On the 11th alone, 115 people were taken to emergency rooms. As heatwaves intensify every summer, the number of 119 emergency medical service dispatches and hospital transport cases for heat-related illnesses has increased by approximately fourfold in the last four years. This month, the scale of human casualties is growing, with 115 heat-related illness patients occurring in a single day. On the afternoon of the 11th, in the yard of a house in Yeongdong, Chungcheongbuk Province...

summary.phenomenon: In the last four years, the number of 119 emergency medical service dispatches and hospital transport cases for heat-related illness reports has increased by approximately fourfold. Notably, on the 11th of this month alone, 115 heat-related illness patients occurred in areas including Yeongdong, Chungcheongbuk Province, and the proportion of elderly patients aged 65 or older was high.
summary.cause: The increase in heat-related illnesses is attributed to heatwaves that intensify every summer.
summary.outlook: The scale of human casualties due to heat-related illnesses is continuously growing.

articles.original_description: 65세 이상 고령 환자 비중 최고지난 11일 하루 115명 응급실행폭염이 여름마다 기승을 부리면서 최근 4년 새 온열질환 신고를 받고 출동한 119 구급활동 건수, 병원 이송 건수가 4배가량 증가한 것으로 나타났다. 이달 들어서는 하루에만 온열질환자 115명이 발생하는 등 인명피해 규모가 갈수록 커지고 있다. 지난 11일 오후에도 충북 영동의 한 주택 마당···

articles.original_title: UK in ‘firewave’ as extreme heat provides ideal conditions for wildfires, experts warn
articles.description: National Fire Chiefs Council say emergency services dealing with 19 wildfires across Britain, many near urban areasThe UK is in the grip of a “firewave”, as the summer’s extreme heat produces the ideal conditions for wildfires, scientists and environmentalists have warned.A particular danger was that more blazes seem to be taking place closer to urban areas rather than in remote countryside, causing hazards to homes and health, they said. Continue reading...

articles.description: Pete and Fran Gillam confirmed dead as authorities use DNA samples to identify victims of blaze in AlmeríaA British couple have been named among the 13 people killed by wildfires in Spain, as authorities race to use DNA to identify victims who were unable to escape the blaze.Pete and Fran Gillam, who lived in Bédar, the village that bore the brunt of the wildfires on Thursday, were confirmed dead by their family.
